Cicerone Exam Prep: The Brewer's Odyssey

The Only Story-Driven Study Guide for the Certified Beer Server and Certified Cicerone Exams

Overview

About This Book

The Cicerone Certification Program is the industry standard for beer knowledge and service — from the entry-level Certified Beer Server to the prestigious Certified Cicerone. But most Cicerone study materials are dense reference guides that read like brewing manuals. Maxwell Pepper's Cicerone Exam Prep: The Brewer's Odyssey follows a traveler journeying through a world where every inn, brewery, and tavern teaches a new concept. You absorb beer knowledge naturally through narrative — and remember it when it matters on exam day.

What This Guide Covers

  1. Beer Styles
  2. Brewing Ingredients & Process
  3. Draught System Maintenance
  4. Off-Flavors
  5. Beer & Food Pairing
Get It on Amazon ↗
Topics

What's Covered

Beer Styles

World beer styles from German lagers to Belgian ales to American IPAs — flavor profiles, history, and how the exam tests style recognition.

Brewing Ingredients & Process

Malt, hops, yeast, and water chemistry. The brewing process from mash to fermentation to conditioning, and how ingredients create flavors.

Draught System Maintenance

How draught systems work, proper line cleaning procedures, gas blends, troubleshooting, and the service standards the Cicerone exam tests.

Off-Flavors

Identification, causes, and prevention of the most common beer off-flavors — a significant portion of the Certified Cicerone exam.

Beer & Food Pairing

Principles of complementary and contrasting pairings, classic beer-food combinations, and how to communicate pairings professionally.

Is This For You?

Who This Book Helps

  • ✓Bartenders and servers pursuing the Certified Beer Server designation
  • ✓Beer enthusiasts and hospitality professionals aiming for the full Certified Cicerone
  • ✓Craft beer bar and restaurant staff seeking to elevate service standards
  • ✓Anyone who finds traditional Cicerone study materials too dry to finish

Why This Book Is Different

Cicerone study materials are notoriously dense. The Brewer's Odyssey structures all of the content into a traveler's journey where every stop in the story world teaches a testable concept. You finish the book having covered all exam content — without feeling like you just read a brewing textbook.

Ready to Get Started?

Available now on Amazon in paperback and Kindle format.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions

Does this cover both Certified Beer Server and Certified Cicerone?
Yes. The guide covers the material for both levels. The Certified Beer Server is the entry-level credential. The Certified Cicerone goes deeper into brewing, styles, and service. This guide prepares you for both.
Do I need professional beer industry experience?
Not for the Certified Beer Server. The Certified Cicerone requires demonstrated beer knowledge and is recommended for those with industry experience, but there is no formal experience requirement.
How long should I study for the Certified Beer Server?
Most candidates are ready in 1-2 weeks of reading and review. The Certified Cicerone requires more depth — plan for 4-8 weeks including practical tastings and experience with draught systems.
Is Cicerone recognized by employers?
Yes. The Cicerone Certification Program is widely recognized in the craft beer industry, hospitality, and restaurant sectors. The Certified Beer Server is increasingly expected at quality craft beer bars.
What is the exam format for the Certified Beer Server?
The Certified Beer Server exam is an online proctored test with multiple-choice questions. The Certified Cicerone involves a written exam plus a tasting component evaluated by Cicerone staff.
